title: Response Bytes, Content Codings, Character Encodings, and Source Identity · Program Brief: Source Digest Provenance
summary: The parser receives truncated compressed bytes, guesses the platform encoding, ignores a conflicting declaration, and hashes normalized text as if it were the source. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the source digest provenance increment in byte-to-Unicode admission ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theor…
objectives: Retain effective URL, response metadata, byte digest, decode decision, parser choice, retrieval time, and fixture or authorization identity.
Reject or separately route images, archives, PDFs, XML, JSON, binary downloads, and mislabeled bodies using type and content evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-bytes-encoding-admission-4
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-bytes-encoding-admission-4.
# Case 1655: a local-server failure rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict.
def worked_scraper_bytes_encoding_admission_4(wire_bytes: int = 48000, expanded_bytes: int = 120000, byte_budget: int = 250000, decoded_pages: int = 6, visible_decode_failures: int = 2) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "authorizationworked4",
        "url1655",
        "response4dekrow",
        "treeworked41655",
        "frontier1655worked4",
        "report4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if wire_bytes < 0 or expanded_bytes < wire_bytes:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "byte-accounting"
    if expanded_bytes > byte_budget:
        return False, expanded_bytes, "expansion-budget"
    if decoded_pages < 1:
        return False, decoded_pages, "decode-coverage"
    if visible_decode_failures < 1:
        return False, visible_decode_failures, "decode-evidence"
    assert expanded_bytes <= byte_budget
    return True, expanded_bytes, "bounded-decoding-ledger"

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Sequence Source Digest Provenance evidence · Theory Source Digest Pr…, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Source Digest Provenance precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Sequence Source Digest Provenance evidence · Theory Source Digest Pr…, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 5007 during a clean package and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile compressed and expanded bytes, character decisions, non-HTML rejection, and source identity; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain effective URL, response metadata, byte digest, decode decision, parser choice, retrieval time, and fixture or authorization identity.

For Authorized crawling and Python Sequence Source Digest Provenance evidence · Theory Source Digest Pr…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: A normalized text hash is enough to reproduce the fetched source.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Source Digest Provenance model · Theory Source Digest Pro…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Source Digest Provenance: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 3388 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile compressed and expanded bytes, character decisions, non-HTML rejection, and source identity; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then inject one retry, cancellation, queue, checkpoint, or output failure and recover cleanly. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain effective URL, response metadata, byte digest, decode decision, parser choice, retrieval time, and fixture or authorization identity.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Source Digest Provenance model · Theory Source Digest Pro….
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Source Digest Provenance model · Theory Source Digest Pro…, keep Source Digest Provenance connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Retain effective URL, response metadata, byte digest, decode decision, parser choice, retrieval time, and fixture or authorization identity.
